name: Four-Way Layer Rotation
summary: Rotate the matrix layer by layer, cycling four cells at a time around each ring.
visualizationIntent: init: the matrix is shown; loop(layer): left/right bound the current ring; loop(offset): the four cells being cycled this step light up as a group (special) â€” top edge, left edge, bottom edge, right edge â€” and their values flash as each moves into the next corner's slot; return: matrix holds the rotated image after every ring is processed.

lineExplanations.3: Measure n, the number of rows and columns.
lineExplanations.4: left marks the outermost layer's low boundary.
lineExplanations.5: right marks the outermost layer's high boundary.
lineExplanations.6: While the current layer still has width, process it.
lineExplanations.7: Step an offset along the layer's top edge.
lineExplanations.8: top is this layer's top row index.
lineExplanations.9: bottom is this layer's bottom row index.
lineExplanations.10: Save the top-left cell before it is overwritten.
lineExplanations.11: Move the bottom-left cell up into the top-left.
lineExplanations.12: Move the bottom-right cell into the bottom-left.
lineExplanations.13: Move the top-right cell into the bottom-right.
lineExplanations.14: Drop the saved top-left value into the top-right.
lineExplanations.15: Shrink the layer inward from the left.
lineExplanations.16: Shrink the layer inward from the right.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class the platform runs.
syntaxExplanations.2: def rotate(self, matrix): the method receiving the n x n matrix.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(matrix) is the number of rows, n.
syntaxExplanations.4: left = 0 starts at the outer layer.
syntaxExplanations.5: right = n - 1 is the last index.
syntaxExplanations.6: while left < right loops over concentric layers.
syntaxExplanations.7: range(right - left) covers each edge position except the final corner.
syntaxExplanations.8: top = left: the top row equals the layer index.
syntaxExplanations.9: bottom = right: the bottom row equals the layer's high boundary.
syntaxExplanations.10: matrix[top][left + offset] reads the top-edge cell.
syntaxExplanations.11: assignment moves the bottom-left cell into the top-left.
syntaxExplanations.12: moves the bottom-right cell into the bottom-left.
syntaxExplanations.13: moves the top-right cell into the bottom-right.
syntaxExplanations.14: places the saved value into the top-right.
syntaxExplanations.17: return matrix hands back the rotated grid.
